Petition Fo Instructions That No Contest Provision
Off Calendar Jan-08-2007 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Determination Of Authenticity & Validity Of Purported Amendment To Trust Agreement
Off Calendar Jan-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Mary Mayo appearing for trustee Maureen Green. George McNitt appearing for Keona Mulgrew. Petition for instructions has been resolved. It should go off calendar. It is before the Court for determination of authenticity of the trust agreement only. Mr. McNitt has not filed his motion to consolidate yet. He was told by the Civil clerk to use the Probate number. Trial in the Civil action is set on 4/9/07. This matter is referred to the Presiding Judge to set for trial. Order signed.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

Petition Fo Instructions That No Contest Provision
Off Calendar Nov-27-2006 Continued To Jan-08-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: George McNitt appearing for Keona Mulgrew. Mary Mayo appearing for trustee Muareen Green. Ms. Mayo requested for refer out for trial. With parties having settlement discussion, Mr. McNitt has not filed a Motion for Consolidation. Trial will take more than two one-half day. They will have the same witnesses for the civil action. Trial on the civil action is set on 4/9/07. Matter is continued to 1/8/2007 for status. Counsel to check with PJ's clerk on how to consolidate. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Susan Lee CSR#4280. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

Petition Fo Instructions That No Contest Provision
Off Calendar Oct-30-2006 Continued To Nov-27-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Terence Mayo (Counsel for Maureen Green); George McNitt (Counsel for Keona Mulgrew).

At the outset, the Court noted that it was in long-standing receipt of a Stipulation and Order re: No Contest Provision which all agreed could be signed by the Court and returned to counsel, which was accomplished in open court. After extensive discussion concerning the status of the pending matter (validity of the amendment to the trust) and a pending civil claim, the Court continued the matter to November 27, 2006 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for status with counsel to discuss the best manner in which to proceed with the case. In addition, the Court elected not to consolidate the two cases at this time and noted that a formal Motion to Consolidate would be required.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722 ; Bailiff: None; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

Petition Fo Instructions That No Contest Provision
Off Calendar May-10-2006 Continued To Aug-09-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Terence Mayo appearing for petitioner. George McNitt appearing for respondent. The respondent has changed her mind about the mediation agreement. Mr. Mayo wants to try mediation again with Bette Epstein. Mr. McNitt can not agree to it but he will not oppose it. His client wants new evaluation of the property. She feels the appraisal is high. Matter is continued to 8/9 for status. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.
